evidence.code_snippet: Imports: bot, config, database, heartbeat, logger, scheduler, service, subproxy, web, xui
evidence.finding: Layered architecture: cmd -> handlers (bot) -> business logic (service) -> data access (database, xui)

evidence.code_snippet: func (h *Handler) HandleUpdate(ctx context.Context, update tgbotapi.Update) { switch { case update.Message.IsCommand(): case update.Callback != nil: default: } }
evidence.finding: HandleUpdate routes: command messages to handlers, callback queries to callbacks, plain messages to menu/interaction

evidence.code_snippet: type Service struct { db *gorm.DB } func (s *Service) CreateSubscription() error { s.db.Create() }
evidence.finding: GORM-based Service with auto-migration via golang-migrate. CRUD operations for Subscription model.

evidence.code_snippet: func (c *Client) Login() error { req, _ := http.NewRequest(...); c.httpClient.Do(req) }
evidence.finding: HTTP client with cookie jar for session. JSON API calls to 3x-ui panel endpoints.

evidence.code_snippet: func (s *SubscriptionService) CreateSubscription() (*database.Subscription, error) { xuiClient.AddClient() -> dbService.CreateSubscription() -> return subURL }
evidence.finding: Flow: validate input -> xui.AddClient -> db.Save -> generate URL -> cache.Set -> notify user

evidence.code_snippet: referralEntry{count, dirty} -> Load() from DB -> Increment/Decrement in memory -> Save() to DB on sync
evidence.finding: In-memory map with single Entry struct (count+dirty merged). Hourly sync via ticker. Dirty tracking for persistence.

evidence.code_snippet: type SubscriptionCache struct { items map[int64]*list.Element; lru *list.List } // O(1) via MoveToBack/PushBack
evidence.finding: container.List for O(1) LRU. Get moves to back (MRU), maxSize evict from front (LRU). TTL cleanup.

evidence.code_snippet: type TokenBucket struct { tokens, refillRate float64 } func (tb *TokenBucket) Allow() bool { if tb.tokens >= 1 { tb.tokens--; return true } }
evidence.finding: Per-user token bucket with configurable rate. Tokens consumed on each message. Refilled per second.

evidence.code_snippet: func (cb *CircuitBreaker) Execute() error { if cb.failures > threshold { return ErrCircuitOpen } cb.failures++ }
evidence.finding: Failures count threshold (5). If exceeded, circuit opens for 30s. Automatic reset after timeout.

evidence.code_snippet: func (s *Server) RegisterChecker(name string, fn func(ctx) ComponentHealth) { s.checkers[name] = fn }
evidence.finding: HTTP server with health/ready endpoints. ComponentHealth checkers for DB, xui. Optional trial page template.

evidence.code_snippet: func MergeSubscriptions(original []byte, extra [][]string, format Format) []byte { combined := append(original, extra...); return FormatOutput(combined, format) }
evidence.finding: Merges base subscription with extra servers. Supports plain and base64 formats. Cached response with 240s TTL.

evidence.code_snippet: func (s *BackupScheduler) Start(ctx context.Context) { ticker := time.NewTicker(24 * time.Hour); for { select { case <-ticker.C: s.backup() } }
evidence.finding: Daily backup at 3AM. WAL checkpoint before copy. 14-day retention. Separate trial cleanup scheduler.

evidence.code_snippet: func Load() (*Config, error) { godotenv.Load(); viper.Unmarshal(&config); return config, nil
evidence.finding: Viper + godotenv. Required fields: XUI credentials, Telegram token, DB path. Validation on load.

evidence.code_snippet: type MockDatabaseService struct { Subscriptions map[int64]*database.Subscription } // Functional mocks for testing
evidence.finding: Centralized mocks in testutil. 80% coverage. Property-based tests for uuid. Golden files for subproxy.

evidence.code_snippet: FROM golang:1.25-alpine AS builder; RUN go build -o /app/bot ./cmd/bot
evidence.finding: Multi-stage build. Alpine base. Health check in compose. SQLite data volume mount.

evidence.code_snippet: func recoverAndReport(component string) { if r := recover(); r != nil { sentry.CurrentHub().Recover(r); logger.Error(...) } }
evidence.finding: Panic recovery wraps goroutines. Sentry capture with stack trace. Zap structured logging.
